title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Execution Trace: Durable Subscription Backlog
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must prove one queue per responsibility, late-subscriber behavior, cancellation, and independent consumer ownership; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the durable subscription backlog increment in a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map. Learners…
objectives: Design durable subscription queues with retention, offline backlog, capacity, replay alternatives, and ownership limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Durable Subscription Backlog · Theory Durable Subscription Backlog
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 8139 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Durable Subscription Backlog contract for the supplied data.
